ground_truth.title: Top 10 Event Planning Trends to Expect in 2025
ground_truth.author: attendease24
ground_truth.main_content:
What does 2025 hold for the events industry? Thanks to rapid innovation and the global sharing of ideas entrenched in technology, event managers and planners have started experimenting with new methods and ideas in order to create the perfect blueprint for their events. At the same time, the core of what makes a successful event remains the same: events are about the experience, from start to finish (and beyond). People want to feel connected, they want to be entertained, and they want valuable content to take away with them. Let's take a look at the top event trends that will shape 2025!

Trend #1 – First-party data from events: With the demise of third-party cookies, first-party data captured at events has become marketing gold. This data allows planners to intimately understand their audiences' interests, preferences, and behaviors. In turn, this intelligence helps them better personalize and improve the event experience.

Trend #2 – Activity-based or experiential events: Pickleball and axe-throwing are no longer just for breweries! In 2025, we'll continue to see more activity-based events. Audiences don't just want to sit back and watch but will instead seek ways to interact with the event.

Trend #3 – Artificial intelligence (AI) technology: AI is poised to transform the events industry in several exciting ways including personalization at scale, data-driven enhancements, and automated administrative tasks.

Trend #4 – Different themes for different rooms: For larger events involving multiple rooms, creating a different theme for each room is an innovative way to curate a truly unforgettable experience for attendees.

Trend #5 – Personalization across the entire event experience: Personalization is key to event success. Event managers and planners can offer personalization across the entire event experience using data-driven insights, advanced technology, and artificial intelligence.

Trend #6 – Advanced event technology: Event technology platforms continue to advance, with new platforms and upgraded data-based technology used in events across industries.

Trend #7 – Diversity, equity, & inclusion: DEI initiatives will continue to be crucial for successful events in 2025. The future of event planning is set to embrace cultural richness and diversity.

Trend #8 – Accessibility for all: Accessibility is more than a trend—it's a requirement. Event managers, venues, and planners are centering accessibility with automatic closed captioning, sensory areas, and sign language interpreters.

Trend #9 – Sustainability as a foundation: In 2025, sustainability remains a top priority for event planners, managers, and attendees. From making venues more sustainable and reducing waste to offering plant-based menus, the events industry will keep the spotlight on sustainability.

Trend #10 – Immersive technologies & experiences: Immersive technologies like augmented reality (AR) and virtual reality (VR) make it possible to transform how attendees, speakers, exhibitors, and guests experience events.
with: With the demise of third-party cookies, first-party data captured at events has become marketing gold.
Audiences don't just want to sit back and watch but will instead seek ways to interact with the event.
AI-powered recommendation engines and chatbots will allow event planners to deliver hyper-personalized experiences to each attendee.
